title: Decision Leverage

content.question: What's the highest-leverage decision in front of you, and are you giving it the right weight?
content.life_lens: Where you live, who you marry, your habits.
sop: List every decision currently in front of you this month.
Rate each on potential Outcome impact, 1-10 (see calculation, page 4), how much it could change your trajectory if it goes well or badly.
Rank them by that score, not by urgency or how loud they are.
Give the top 10% real deliberation time, research, advice, sleep on it, before deciding.
Act decisively once decided; low-leverage decisions don't deserve the same hesitation.
questions: What's the highest-leverage decision in front of you, and are you giving it the right weight?
Which decision from the last year had the biggest downstream effect, and how much time did you actually spend on it before deciding?
Are you spending equal time on a $50 choice and a $50,000 one?
content.equation_example: Weight (how much this decision matters, rate 1-10) times Outcome (how much it could change your trajectory, rate 1-10) equals Impact score. Weight 9 times Outcome 8 equals 72. Higher means this decision deserves real deliberation time; lower means it's fine to decide fast and move on.
content.closing_statement: Give your biggest decisions the time they deserve.

caption:
Principle Nº 183: Decision Leverage

Few decisions create most outcomes.

Ask yourself: What's the highest-leverage decision in front of you, and are you giving it the right weight?
